summary: Six user-reported UX/budget fixes: F42 max_turns honors -1=unlimited (symmetric with time/tokens/cost; guarded budget_violation/warning/child_budgets/status/composer); F43 engine+statemap accept 'state' key in phase.json so the live state diagram tracks recipes (e.g. self-audit) that use it; F36 conversation permission edits no longer 409 during an active reply (apply next reply, like budgets); F37 audit note-for-next-run draft cleared BEFORE reload so it doesn't refill; F41 conversation composer gains a live '✕ stop' (abort) button; F38 decision/question answer fields are textareas with Enter=submit/Shift+Enter=newline. +3 tests.

Follow-up user work order (fix F39 + D8). F39: POST /api/questions/{qid}/answer is now async and, after filing the answer, resumes a FINISHED conversation in place (runner.resume, as message() does) so the engine's collect_deferred_answers actually consumes the answer — a live reply/scheduled routine is untouched. D8 (user framing: 'one is the permission, the other the means of asking for it'): added grants.floor_capabilities and applied raise+floor in resolve_permission_layers so a gated action / reserved util / run access survives ONLY as the means of a HELD conduct permission (confirm level + run depth kept as user policy); permissions.js gains the inverse cascade (enabling a capability holds its covering permission) so the panel can't express a contradiction. +3 tests, updated 1 (the cascade test now asserts the floor). pytest 480 passed / 3 skipped, rsched-lint green.

Versioning hygiene (user work order). Bumped __version__ 0.15.0 -> 0.16.0 for the changes shipped since 0.15.0 (Mark's max_cost/wall-clock commit 4bf63bd5bd + this run's 56d620dbe3 and c6ca03ffa8) that had landed without a version bump. Added a comprehensive root CHANGELOG.md (Keep a Changelog + SemVer): detailed sections for 0.16.0 and the tagged releases 0.15.0/0.14.1/0.14.0/0.13.1, plus a thematic reconstruction of pre-0.13.1 development from git history. The version comment already mandated 'bump the minor on every user-facing revision' — the gate that was being skipped. pytest 480 passed / 3 skipped (status test reads __version__ dynamically); rsched-lint green.

Follow-up user order 'remove the orphans and implement the rest'. Implemented both filed recipe recommendations in ~/routines (outside the scheduler repo): q-152 — routine-improver/steps/study-target.md now reads each target's audit/report.json findings + settled/answered decisions (decisions-answered.json, inbox/answer-*.json) + open deferred questions, and scans the full LEDGER for recipe-change work orders (committed in the routine-improver git repo); q-144 — self-audit/steps/act-apply-fixes.md now requires a __version__ bump + CHANGELOG.md entry + (x.y.z) subject tag on every user-facing scheduler commit (auto-committed by the engine with self-audit's dir). Removed 2 orphaned decision artifacts (self-audit/inbox/answer-q-20260711-082732-35.json; runs/.../sub/2/questions/pending/q-...-31.json). This scheduler commit is audit/ bookkeeping only (report.json gains findings F45+F46, changelog.jsonl this line) — no code or version change.
